title: Bomb squad called to Scottish military base as 'suspicious package' found

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The scene at RAF Lossiemouth on Thursday (Image: Peter Jolly) A BOMB squad was called to a military base in Scotland after a "suspicious package" was found. A 200m cordon was put in place around RAF Lossiemouth on Thursday afternoon, with civilians asked to avoid the area. Military personnel from the Moray base had been participating in an exercise this week, dubbed "Exercise Agile Warrior", but an RAF spokesperson confirmed that the incident is not part of this.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Emergency services were first made aware of the discovery at around 11.30am on Thursday. At around 3.30pm, an armoured vehicle was seen entering the gates of the base, with an inspection of the package expected to take place. A spokesperson for RAF Lossiemouth said: “We can confirm that during a routine security search at RAF Lossiemouth, a suspicious package was found.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: “EOD (Explosive Ordnance Disposal) have been notified, and a cordon is in place. This is not part of the previously advertised exercise activity.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Bomb squad called to Scottish military base after 'suspicious package' found
